beating.definition: Here in 'beating yourself up' means criticizing yourself very harshly.
beating.example: Stop beating yourself up over small mistakes.

adopt.definition: To start doing or using something, especially a habit or way of behaving.
adopt.example: Children may adopt the same bad habits as their parents.

potentially.definition: Possibly; with the chance of happening.
potentially.example: This is a potentially damaging habit.

mirror.definition: A piece of glass that shows a reflection of what is in front of it.
mirror.example: She looked in the mirror and criticized herself.

picking.definition: Here in 'picking fault' means finding and pointing out mistakes or flaws.
picking.example: She was always picking fault with herself.

fault.definition: A mistake or weakness in someone or something.
fault.example: She could always find a fault in herself.

exact.definition: The same in every detail; precisely the same.
exact.example: Years later, she did the exact same thing.

astute.definition: Very quick to notice and understand things; clever.
astute.example: Her astute teenage daughter noticed the behavior.

inherited.definition: Received from parents or earlier generations through behavior or example.
inherited.example: Our inherited patterns shape how we behave.

patterns.definition: Regular and repeated ways of behaving.
patterns.example: We repeat patterns we learned as children.

fault-finder.definition: A voice or habit that always looks for things that are wrong.

continual.definition: Happening again and again without stopping.
continual.example: There is continual chatter in our heads.

chatter.definition: Talk that goes on and on, often without much meaning.
chatter.example: The chatter in your mind can be very negative.

commentary.definition: A running explanation or description of what is happening.
commentary.example: We have a continual commentary running in our heads.

harsh.definition: Cruel and unkind; very strict and critical.
harsh.example: The harsh inner critic makes you feel terrible.

critic.definition: A voice (real or in your mind) that finds fault and judges negatively.
critic.example: Your inner critic can hold you back.

hopeless.definition: Feeling that there is no chance of success; without hope.
hopeless.example: She told herself she was hopeless.

useless.definition: Not able to do anything well; having no value.
useless.example: The inner voice told her she was useless.

steer.definition: To guide or influence the direction something goes.
steer.example: That inner talk has a powerful steer on your life.

impact.definition: A strong effect or influence on something.
impact.example: Your self-talk has an impact on your child.

influencing.definition: Having an effect on how someone thinks, feels, or acts.
influencing.example: Your habits are influencing your children.

judgements.definition: Opinions or decisions about whether something is good or bad.
judgements.example: Harmful judgements can damage a child's confidence.

harmful.example: Negative self-talk is harmful to everyone.

exaggerate.definition: To make something seem bigger, worse, or more important than it really is.
exaggerate.example: The inner voice can exaggerate a low mood.

mood.definition: The way you feel at a particular time.
mood.example: A negative inner voice can worsen a bad mood.

knock.definition: Here means to reduce or weaken something.

inadequate.definition: Not good enough; not having what is needed.
inadequate.example: The voice makes us feel generally inadequate.

capacity.definition: The ability or power to do or experience something.
capacity.example: Everyone has the capacity for happiness.

happiness.definition: The state of feeling joy and pleasure.
happiness.example: The inner critic can block your capacity for happiness.

fundamental.definition: The most basic and important part of something.
fundamental.example: Childhood experience is the fundamental way we develop.

shake.definition: Here in 'shake off' means to get rid of something that bothers you.
shake.example: It is hard to shake off old habits.

critical.example: The critical voice in your head is hard to stop.

pat.definition: Here in 'pat on the back' means praise or recognition for doing well.
pat.example: Give yourself a pat on the back for noticing.

gallery.definition: A building or room where art is displayed for people to look at.
gallery.example: She works as an art-gallery assistant.

assistant.definition: A person who helps someone with their work.
assistant.example: She works as a gallery assistant.

aware.definition: Knowing about something; having knowledge of it.
aware.example: She is aware of her inner negative voice.

failure.definition: Not succeeding at something you tried to do.
failure.example: She was always afraid of failure.

embarrass.definition: To make someone feel shy, uncomfortable, or ashamed in front of others.
embarrass.example: She was afraid she would embarrass herself.

dissuade.definition: To talk someone out of doing something; to persuade them not to do it.
dissuade.example: The inner voice dissuades her from trying new things.

criticize.definition: To say what is wrong with something or someone.
criticize.example: She would criticize herself for not being adventurous.

unadventurous.definition: Not willing to try new or exciting things.
unadventurous.example: She told herself she was unadventurous.

applying.definition: Here means making an effort or working hard at something.
applying.example: She criticized herself for not applying herself.

shallow.definition: Not deep in thought or feeling; only caring about surface things.
shallow.example: She told herself she was shallow.

passion.definition: A very strong feeling of love or interest in something.
passion.example: She felt she had no real passion for anything.

expertise.definition: Special skill or knowledge in a particular area.
expertise.example: She believed she had no expertise in anything.

guilty.definition: Feeling bad because you think you have done something wrong.
guilty.example: She felt guilty blaming her mother.

worrier.definition: A person who worries a lot about things.
worrier.example: Her mother has always been a worrier.

negativity.definition: A tendency to focus on the bad parts of things.
negativity.example: Her mother's negativity affected the whole family.

compliment.definition: A nice thing said about someone or something they did.
compliment.example: She can never take a compliment.

delicious.definition: Very tasty and enjoyable to eat.

lasagne.definition: An Italian dish made with layers of pasta, meat sauce, and cheese.
lasagne.example: She made a lasagne for dinner.

flavour.definition: The taste of food or drink.
flavour.example: She said the lasagne had no flavour.

dwell.definition: To think about something for too long, especially something negative.
dwell.example: We dwell on our failures instead of moving on.

evidence.definition: Information that shows whether something is true or false.
evidence.example: She used failures as evidence she was no good.

bother.definition: To make an effort to do something; also can mean to worry about something.
bother.example: She felt she shouldn't even bother trying.

fitting.definition: A session where you try on clothes to see if they fit properly, especially for a wedding dress.
fitting.example: At the final fitting, she tried on her wedding dress.

wedding.definition: The ceremony where two people get married.
wedding.example: She was excited about her wedding day.

pursed.definition: Pressed together tightly, usually the lips, showing worry or disapproval.
pursed.example: Her mother pursed her lips and looked worried.

veil.definition: A piece of thin fabric worn over the face or head, often by a bride.
veil.example: With the veil and flowers, the dress looked perfect.

unwittingly.definition: Without knowing or meaning to; accidentally.
unwittingly.example: She unwittingly passed on her anxiety to her children.

anxiety.definition: A feeling of worry and nervousness about what might happen.
anxiety.example: Her mother's anxiety affected everyone around her.

insecurities.definition: Feelings of not being good enough or of being uncertain about yourself.
insecurities.example: Her mother's insecurities showed in her comments.

lay waste.example: Her anxiety can lay waste to the people around her.

self-torturous.definition: Causing pain and suffering to yourself in your own mind.
self-torturous.example: Her self-torturous inner critic was always active.

demonize.definition: To make someone seem very bad or evil.
demonize.example: I don't want to demonize her mother.

unaware.definition: Not knowing about something; not noticing.
unaware.example: She was unaware of how her self-talk affected her children.

determined.definition: Having a strong decision to do something and not giving up.
determined.example: She was determined not to pass it on.

demoralizing.definition: Making you lose hope and confidence.

argue.definition: To disagree with someone using words; to fight with words.
argue.example: She used to argue with the inner voice.

engage.definition: To get involved with something; to give it your attention.
engage.example: Don't engage with the negative voice.

colleague.example: Treat the inner critic like a difficult work colleague.

entitled.example: You are entitled to your opinion.

override.definition: To use your will to go against something; to overpower.
override.example: She makes herself override her fears.

discourage.definition: To make someone feel less confident or willing to do something.
discourage.example: Don't let fear discourage your children.

unexpected.example: An unexpected side effect was more confidence.

side effect.definition: An extra result that happens in addition to the main one.
side effect.example: The unexpected side effect was feeling better about life.

separate.example: Let's separate what she did into steps.

content.definition: Here means the substance or meaning of what someone is doing.
content.example: If we separate the content into a process, it goes like this.

process.definition: A series of steps or actions taken to achieve something.

awkward.definition: Difficult to deal with; making you feel uncomfortable.
awkward.example: Treat the voice like someone awkward.

acknowledge.definition: To accept or recognize that something exists or is true.
acknowledge.example: Acknowledge what the voice says but don't agree.

colluding.definition: Secretly working together with someone, usually in a bad way; agreeing with something harmful.
colluding.example: Don't collude with the inner critic.

expand.definition: To make something bigger or wider.
expand.example: Expand your comfort zone by trying new things.

comfort zone.definition: The range of activities where you feel safe and relaxed.
comfort zone.example: Growth happens when you step outside your comfort zone.

self-doubt.definition: A feeling of not being sure about your own abilities.
self-doubt.example: Self-doubt can be overcome with practice.

creeps.definition: Moves slowly and quietly; here means gradually appears.
creeps.example: Self-doubt creeps in when you are tired.

incentive.definition: Something that motivates you to do something.
incentive.example: Protecting your child is an extra incentive to change.

mindful.definition: Being careful and aware of something.
mindful.example: Be mindful of your inner critic.

reveal.definition: To show or make something known that was hidden.
reveal.example: The exercise helps reveal your inner critic.

pencil.definition: A thin writing tool, usually made of wood with a dark center.
pencil.example: Keep a pencil and pad nearby.

pad.definition: A number of sheets of paper held together for writing notes.
pad.example: Write your thoughts on a pad.

self-critical.definition: Being very hard on yourself; always finding your own faults.
self-critical.example: Note down any self-critical thoughts during the day.

articulate.definition: To express thoughts or feelings clearly in words.
articulate.example: Do you recognize these criticisms as ones you have seen others articulate?

achieve.definition: To succeed in doing something you worked for.
achieve.example: Think of something you would like to achieve.

steps.definition: Individual actions taken one at a time toward a goal.
steps.example: What steps do you need to take?

remind.definition: To make someone think of something they might have forgotten.
remind.example: Does this voice remind you of anyone?

vibe.definition: A feeling or atmosphere that someone or something gives off.
vibe.example: She passed on this not-good-enough vibe to her children.

stick.definition: Here in 'stick at things' means to continue doing something and not give up.
stick.example: She tells herself she doesn't stick at things.
